title: Cenchrus henryanus
taxonomy: Cenchrus henryanus is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Cenchrus, which comprises several species of spiny grasses. The genus Cenchrus is further classified within the subfamily Chloridoideae. This subfamily is a part of the large and diverse family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. Cenchrus henryanus is native to Australia and is often found in dry, sandy regions.

preparations: Cenchrus henryanus is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific, including parts of Australia and the Pacific Islands. intro: Cenchrus henryanus, commonly known as the Hawaiian sandbur, is a perennial grass native to Hawaii and other Pacific Island regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including inflammation and infection, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Cenchrus henryanus include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Cenchrus henryanus is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
